messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
Date of the first goal scored by wayne rooney in FIFA Club World Cup

content.message:
I need to find the date of the first goal scored by Wayne Rooney in the FIFA Club World Cup.

Let me think through this step by step:

1. First, I need to identify Wayne Rooney as an entity in Wikidata
2. Then find information about his goals in the FIFA Club World Cup
3. Specifically looking for the date of his first goal in that competition

Let me search for Wayne Rooney in Wikidata first to get his entity identifier.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Wayne Rooney (wd:Q266613), also known as "Wayne Mark Rooney":
  - English footballer and manager
  - has occupation association football coach
  - has occupation association football player
  - is instance of human
2. Kai Rooney (wd:Q118385233, matched via "Kai Wayne Rooney"), also known as "Kai Wayne Rooney":

  - has occupation association football player

content.message:
I've found Wayne Rooney in Wikidata with the identifier wd:Q266613. Now I need to find information about his goals in the FIFA Club World Cup.

Let me search for properties related to goals or matches for Wayne Rooney specifically in the context of the FIFA Club World Cup.

tool_calls.name: search_property_of_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. child (wd:P40 as wdt/p, matched via "issue"), also known as "children", "daughter", "daughters", "descendants", "father of", etc.:
  - subject has object as child. Do not use for stepchildren—use "relative" (P1038), qualified with "kinship to subject" (P1039)
2. number of children (wd:P1971 as wdt/p, matched via "issue"), also known as "children", "children (number)", "how many children", "issue", "kids", etc.:
  - number of children of the person, animal, or character
3. position played on team / speciality (wd:P413 as wdt/p, matched via "position"), also known as "fielding position", "player position", "position", "position (on team)", "specialism", etc.:
  - position or specialism of a player on a team
4. birth name (wd:P1477 as wdt/p, matched via "nee"), also known as "birthname", "bn", "bname", "born as", "full birth name", etc.:
  - full name of a person at birth, if different from their current, generally used name
5. described by source (wd:P1343 as wdt/p, matched via "entry"), also known as "dbs", "def in", "defined in", "desc by biography", "desc by encyclopedia", etc.:
  - work where this item is described, in statistical contexts, a methodological note describing the data
6. league or competition (wd:P118 as wdt/p, matched via "division"), also known as "competition", "cup", "division", "sports league", "tournament":
  - league or competition in which team or player has played, or in which an event occurs
7. sibling (wd:P3373 as wdt/p, matched via "sis"), also known as "bro", "brother", "brother of", "brother or sister", "brothers", etc.:
  - the subject and the object have at least one common parent ... etc.) and step-siblings (step-brothers, step-sisters, etc.)
8. image (wd:P18 as wdt/p, matched via "still"), also known as "album artwork", "album cover", "artwork", "camera picture", "Commons image", etc.:
  - image of relevant illustration of the subject; if available, ... map, flag image, signature image, logo image, collage image)
9. instance of (wd:P31 as wdt/p, matched via "type"), also known as "example of", "has type", "is a", "is an", "is of type", etc.:
  - type to which this subject corresponds/belongs. Different ... is an instance of mountain; volcano is a subclass of mountain
10. number of matches played/races/starts (wd:P1350 as wdt/p, matched via "starts"), also known as "appearances", "caps", "games pitched", "games played", "gp", etc.:
  - matches or games a player or a team played during an event. ... a player officially appeared in during the whole career

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. has goal (wd:P3712 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "goal"), also known as "aim", "aimed at", "aim to", "designed for", "design goal", etc.:
  - desired result or outcome
2. directs readers to (wd:P13269 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "target"), also known as "directs users to", "is citation of", "links to", "redirect to", "refers to", etc.:
  - document or class of documents to which this item or class directs readers
3. target (wd:P533 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - target of an attack or military operation
4. class of object(s) of occurrence (wd:P12913 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "target"), also known as "acted on", "acts on", "affects item(s) of the class", "applies to item(s) of the class", "class of object of occurrence", etc.:
  - class that includes the object(s) to which this occurrence (or class of occurrence) occurs or occurred
5. object of occurrence (wd:P12912 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "target"), also known as "action applies to item", "affects", "did to", "direct object", "does to", etc.:
  - specific object to which an occurrence or class of occurrences applies
6. destination of transfer (wd:P12694 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "target"), also known as "destination", "end point", "receiver", "recipient", "target", etc.:
  - entity that a transferred item comes to be associated with as a result of this process
7. field of work (wd:P101 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "purpose"), also known as "academic area", "academic discipline", "academic subject", "activity", "activity domain", etc.:
  - specialization of a person or organization; see P106 for the occupation
8. has use (wd:P366 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "purpose"), also known as "as", "can be used for", "for", "function", "had use", etc.:
  - main use of the subject (includes current and former usage)
9. number of points/goals/set scored (wd:P1351 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "goals for"), also known as "GF", "goals for", "goals scored", "number of goals", "number of points", etc.:
  - goals / points scored in a match or an event used as qualifier to the participant. Use P1358 for league points.
10. orientation (wd:P7469 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "direction"), also known as "alignment", "direction", "face", "faces", "facing":
  - axis of alignment of an item, or direction perpendicular to its primary face (specify with P518 if necessary)

8. name day (wd:P1750 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
